judgment.summary: A financial institution with strong revenue and earnings growth, but facing some margin and return pressures, trading at a low forward P/E.
judgment.earningsRecap: Last quarter missed EPS estimates by 3.3%; next earnings date is 2026-07-21.
positives: Revenue growth 46.3% YoY and quarterly earnings growth 54.8% indicate strong top-line momentum.
Forward P/E of 7.63x and PEG ratio of 0.20x suggest the stock is undervalued relative to future earnings.
Analyst consensus is 'buy' with a mean target of $255.19, indicating positive sentiment from the sell-side.
The company pays a dividend with a 1.7% yield, supported by a significant cash balance of $79.39B.
negatives: Profit margin of 8.9% and Return on Equity of 3.3% are modest.
Earnings growth YoY was negative at -3.2%, and current quarter analyst estimates show a decline of -13.2%.
Gross margin is reported at 0.0%, which is unusual and warrants further investigation.
The dividend payout ratio is high at 86.1%, which could be a concern if earnings decline.

news.paragraph: Capital One Financial Corporation (COF) recently saw its board approve a quarterly dividend, coinciding with reported top-line growth. Analyst commentary from TD Cowen offers a perspective on the company's trajectory. The upcoming earnings report on July 21st will be a key event to watch for further insights into performance.

headlines.title: Here’s What TD Cowen Thinks About Capital One Financial Corporation (COF)
